diagnostics.title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
diagnostics.message: "recharts" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
diagnostics.help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

diagnostics.title: Mutation without cache invalidation
diagnostics.message: useMutation with no cache update leaves your users looking at stale data after it runs.
diagnostics.help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

diagnostics.title: Mutation without cache invalidation
diagnostics.message: useMutation with no cache update leaves your users looking at stale data after it runs.
diagnostics.help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

diagnostics.title: Mutation without cache invalidation
diagnostics.message: useMutation with no cache update leaves your users looking at stale data after it runs.
diagnostics.help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

diagnostics.title: Mutation without cache invalidation
diagnostics.message: useMutation with no cache update leaves your users looking at stale data after it runs.
diagnostics.help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "i".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 5 useState calls in "PersonalInfoPanel" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: Your cleanup may read the wrong node since the ref `subRef.current` can change before it runs.

diagnostics.title: State initialized from a mount effect
diagnostics.message: Your users see an extra render with empty "connected" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
diagnostics.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Time or random value in JSX
diagnostics.message: This can cause a hydration mismatch because new Date() reached from JSX gives a different value on the server than in the browser. Move it into useEffect+useState to run only in the browser, or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if it's on purpose.
diagnostics.help: Move time or random values into useEffect+useState so they only run in the browser, or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if it's intentional

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `activeId` & show your users old data.
diagnostics.suppressionHint: oxlint matches plugin rules only by their full name, so `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` in your eslint-disable comment does not silence `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps` — change it to `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps`.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with state plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Derived value copied into state
diagnostics.message: Storing "messages" in state when you can derive it from other values costs an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `map.getCenter, onMove` & show your users old data.
diagnostics.suppressionHint: oxlint matches plugin rules only by their full name, so `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` in your eslint-disable comment does not silence `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps` — change it to `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps`.

diagnostics.title: Data passed to parent via effect
diagnostics.message: Handing data back to a parent from a useEffect costs your users an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Fetch the data in the parent and pass it down as a prop (or return it from the hook), instead of handing it back up through a prop callback in a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#passing-data-to-the-parent

diagnostics.title: Prop derived into useState
diagnostics.message: Your users see a stale value when prop "value" changes because useState copies it once.
diagnostics.help: Compute the value inline so prop changes do not leave `useState` holding a stale copy.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "i".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Multiple setState calls in one effect
diagnostics.message: 4 setState calls in one useEffect redraw your screen each time they run together.
diagnostics.help: Combine related updates in `useReducer` so one effect does not redraw the screen once per `setState` call.

diagnostics.title: Mutation without cache invalidation
diagnostics.message: useMutation with no cache update leaves your users looking at stale data after it runs.
diagnostics.help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `status, transactionId, verify.mutate, setUser, user` & show your users old data.
diagnostics.suppressionHint: oxlint matches plugin rules only by their full name, so `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` in your eslint-disable comment does not silence `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps` — change it to `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps`.

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `useMemo` can run with a stale `act, reject` & show your users old data.
diagnostics.suppressionHint: oxlint matches plugin rules only by their full name, so `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` in your eslint-disable comment does not silence `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps` — change it to `react-doctor/exhaustive-deps`.

diagnostics.title: Custom modal instead of dialog
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can tab out of this `role="dialog"` modal because it has no built-in focus trapping, so use the native `<dialog>`, which gives you focus trapping, `Escape` to close, and the backdrop for free.
diagnostics.help: Replace the wrapper with `<dialog>` and open it with `dialog.showModal()`. For the trigger, prefer `<button commandfor="id" command="show-modal">` (Chrome 135+), or a `useRef` with `dialogRef.current?.showModal()`.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<dialog>` than `role="dialog"`, so use `<dialog>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Time or random value in JSX
diagnostics.message: This can cause a hydration mismatch because new Date() reached from JSX gives a different value on the server than in the browser. Move it into useEffect+useState to run only in the browser, or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if it's on purpose.
diagnostics.help: Move time or random values into useEffect+useState so they only run in the browser, or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if it's intentional

diagnostics.message: Unused export: `isTenant` is exported but no module imports it, so it expands the public surface and can mislead callers about supported API.
diagnostics.help: Drop the `export` keyword (or remove the declaration) if no other module uses this symbol.
